Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €357,350
  • Size: 71.47 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — looks like a “project” listing (old-building vibe + new attic build); readiness hinges on build status, handover date, and what’s actually included in the spec.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / 71 sqm can work for a couple + baby, but it’s not a long-term family layout) Investor: ➖ (good location signals, but project/attic uncertainty + tourism pressure can complicate rental stability) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Leopoldstadt with dense food/transit/parks is a daily-life win)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the micro-location looks strong, but the listing reads like “marketing for a build/product,” so you want clarity (and price protection) before you commit.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €330k–€350k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat this as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Make your offer conditional on (1) confirmed monthly running costs + reserve status, (2) written construction/spec sheet for the attic portion, and (3) proof of quiet orientation (courtyard vs street) via plan + viewing. If they won’t move on price, push for value: turnkey finish level, kitchen credit, or closing-cost contribution.

Bottom line

Leopoldstadt can be an A+ “liveable city” district: you’ve got transit + groceries + parks in short walking distance. The trap is paying “storybook period building + shiny Dachgeschoss” pricing without verifying noise, overheating risk, and the building’s financial health. If the orientation and costs check out, this is the kind of place you’ll enjoy daily, not just on viewing day.

#379
Ihr Stil. Ihre Raeume. Ihr Zuhause. - so individuell wie Sie. (Ihr Stil. Ihre Räume. Ihr Zuhause. - so individuell wie Sie.)
€385,000 · 53.7 m2 · 2 rooms · Schuettelstrasse 75, Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €385,000
  • Size: 53.7 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — too little interior/condition detail; assume “fine but verify” (windows, bath/kitchen age, electrics, moisture, noise).

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (2 rooms / ~54 sqm is tight long-term) Investor: ➖ (good demand area, but price per sqm is punchy; heating setup adds diligence) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (strong central-ish 2nd district feel; river/canal walks + decent transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location fundamentals look solid, but the €385k ask for 53.7 sqm needs the apartment’s condition + building health to justify it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €355k–€375k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push hardest on (1) individual “floor” heating (age/type, servicing history, future replacement), (2) true all-in monthly costs (utilities + heating), (3) building reserve fund + upcoming works, (4) noise (street/canal traffic, nightlife/tourism drift), and (5) any layout inefficiency in a 2-room plan.

Bottom line

If it’s quiet, bright, and recently refreshed: this can be a great “city base” in Leopoldstadt. If it needs systems upgrades (heating/electrics/windows): the current price becomes hard to defend. Buy the building as much as the unit: reserves + planned repairs will make or break the deal.

€389,000 · 36.18 m2 · 1 rooms · Obere Donaustrasse 23, Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Address/map mismatch flagged (likely approximate pin).
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €389,000
  • Size: 36.18 m2
  • Rooms: 1
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — modern spec signals (underfloor heating, balcony, “urban & modern”) but the listing extract lacks condition/photos/fee details.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great central-ish river/canal-adjacent lifestyle potential, but the price-per-sqm looks punchy for a 36 sqm micro-unit unless the building and finishes are truly premium.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €345k–€375k (best-effort guess; comps not provided here, so validate with 3–5 nearby closed/active listings for 25–45 sqm units). Anchor on missing monthly costs + unclear floor + micro-size resale friction; ask for (1) building year/condition, (2) reserve fund status and planned works, (3) elevator presence, (4) exact balcony size/orientation and sun hours, (5) full running-cost breakdown.

Bottom line

This is a “pay for location + outdoor space” apartment — you buy the balcony and the neighborhood more than the layout. At 36 sqm, future resale/rent is sensitive to floor level, light, noise, and building quality — you need those to be excellent. If the floor is low or street noise is real, this becomes a hard pass at this price.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €390,000
  • Size: 82 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — classic “period building near a major node” usually means you need to verify windows/soundproofing, damp risk, and the true condition of plumbing/electrics.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ✔ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (size, quiet claim, garden use, location), but the monthly costs are chunky and “quiet” near this corridor must be proven with windows/orientation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €350k–€380k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Use (1) monthly costs €418.49 as a leverage anchor, (2) any lack of elevator/floor uncertainty, (3) any period building capex signals (wiring, pipes, façade/roof, courtyard) to justify the haircut. Make the offer conditional on document review (reserve fund, last 3 years owner meeting notes, planned works) and a daylight + late-evening noise check.

Bottom line

Good “liveable Vienna” recipe: Prater + serious transit + enough daily amenities to skip the car. The risk is not the apartment — it’s building condition + noise reality + whether the garden use is truly meaningful. At the right price, this is a very rational 2nd district buy.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location signals look good, but the listing is missing the hard numbers (heating, energy, monthly costs) that can swing value materially.

At €399k for 58 sqm, you’re paying for district + building story — don’t pay full price without the operating-cost and technical picture. If the building paperwork is clean and costs are normal, it’s a solid two-room buy; if not, walk fast.

Family: ➖ (3 rooms at ~72 sqm can work for 1 kid, but layout + noise insulation + storage will decide) Investor: ➖ (strong location liquidity, but heating setup + unknown condition can cap rentability) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (quiet 3-room + dense food/drink + parks/transit nearby is a very liveable daily pattern)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location looks solid and the ticket price is attractive for 1020, but “unknowns” (condition, floor, windows, building state, reserves) are exactly where period building deals turn.

Max-Winter- Square area: you’re buying daily life (restaurants + groceries + parks + transit) more than “perfect specs.” If the apartment is truly quiet and has good windows/ceilings, it’s a sleeper hit. If it’s low floor facing a busy side or needs major systems work, you’re paying in stress and capex — price it accordingly.

Buyer fit

If the layout is quiet-to-courtyard and the building’s reserves are healthy, this is a high-liquidity 1020 buy. If it’s street-facing with thin windows + unclear renovation budget, you’ll overpay fast. Make the seller price the uncertainty — don’t.

#385
"Leopold Quartier" Optimal geschnittenes Mikroapartment mit Balkon ( & Provisionsfrei!) Top 87 ("Leopold Quartier" Optimal geschnittenes Mikroapartment mit Balkon (Erstbezug & Provisionsfrei!) Top 87)
€428,000 · 36.18 m2 · 1 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €428,000
  • Size: 36.18 m2
  • Rooms: 1
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new-build first occupancy) — the question is less “condition” and more “is the product right for you”.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (1 room / 36 sqm is fundamentally tight) Investor: ➖ (works only if building rules + rental demand + fees line up) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (if you want a compact, low-maintenance pied-à-terre near parks + transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great micro-location signals, but the price-per-sqm for a micro unit needs to be justified by layout, building quality, and future resale liquidity.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €395k–€415k (best-effort; I don’t have direct comps for this specific project/unit, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Negotiation levers to use: - “Developer inventory” angle: ask how many similar micro units are still available and whether there are incentives (kitchen package, storage unit, parking option, closing-cost contribution) even if headline price is fixed. - Verify the all-in monthly carrying cost: request the full operating-cost breakdown + reserve contribution + any special building service packages. If it’s higher than typical, push price down. - Noise/orientation uncertainty: ask for exact orientation (street/courtyard), balcony exposure, and any planned nearby construction phases; use any ambiguity to negotiate.

Bottom line

- This is a premium “small but polished” product: you’re paying for location, new-build convenience, and balcony — not for space. - The deal goes wrong if resale is thin (micro-units can be fickle) or if the building’s fee structure turns it into an expensive box. - If it’s quiet (courtyard-facing), low fees, and a genuinely efficient layout, it can be a strong lifestyle base or corporate apartment.

#387
TOP 3-rooms-Altbauwohnung beim Praterstern (TOP 3-Zimmer-Altbauwohnung beim Praterstern)
€449,000 · 77 m2 · 3 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €449,000
  • Size: 77 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “period building” + individual heating usually means condition/noise/windows and heating system age matter a lot, and we don’t have floor/renovation details.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (great access + parks nearby, but Praterstern micro-location can be loud/chaotic depending on orientation) Investor: ✔ (high transit demand for 3 rooms; liquidity likely strong if the layout is clean) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (excellent city connectivity + walkable amenities, but you must tolerate the hub energy)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — price can make sense for 77 m² / 3 rooms in the 2nd, but Praterstern-adjacent risk (noise, footfall, building quality variability) needs compensation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €410k–€435k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted target rather than a comp-anchored valuation). Push on: individual heating running costs/maintenance, window quality (street vs courtyard), staircase/lift situation, and any upcoming building works. If it’s rear-facing + recently renovated, you can justify moving closer to ask.

Bottom line

If the living rooms/bedrooms face quiet courtyard and the building is well-run, this is a very “easy to live in / easy to resell” 2nd-district pick. If it faces the hub streets or has tired common areas, you’re buying stress — demand the discount. The neighborhood has strong daily-life convenience (groceries + food density) and elite transit connectivity; that’s the core value here.

#388

€450,000 · 56.5 m2 · 3 rooms · Stuwerstrasse 60, Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €450,000
  • Size: 56.5 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: central heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “revitalised old building” can mean turnkey interiors, but building-level works/finish quality vary a lot by developer.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3 rooms on 56.5 sqm can work, but it’s compact; layout and storage will make or break it) Investor: ✔ (liquid area, strong rental demand drivers nearby; watch yield vs. total monthly costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high walkability: lots of parks + solid food/drink density, plus transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location signal (parks + transit + amenities) but the €450k ticket for 56.5 sqm only works if the renovation spec, building finances, and noise profile check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €410k–€440k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps or monthly building costs here, so this range is conditional). Push hardest on (1) missing monthly costs, (2) any upcoming building works/reserves, (3) proof of renovation scope (roof/façade/common areas vs. just the unit), and (4) street-facing noise.

Bottom line

You’re paying for “close-to-everything” urban living: parks, transit, and a non-tourist-saturated but active pocket. The biggest risk is not the area — it’s the building economics and the true quality of the “revitalisation.” If monthly costs + reserves are clean and the unit isn’t a noise box, it’s a very defensible buy.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — modern comfort signals (underfloor heating) but the listing data is thin (no energy class, no floor, no condition notes), so assume “fine but verify”.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / 55 sqm is tight long-term unless it’s a very specific setup) Investor: ✔ ("Anlegerhit" framing + strong transit count; terrace can help liquidity) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (quiet green view + big terrace is the whole point)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — terrace + quiet green outlook in 1020 can be a killer combo, but at ~€8.5k/sqm you need proof on building quality, operating costs, and rental/exit comps.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €430k–€455k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps here, so treat this as an opening band, not a valuation). Use the “missing facts” as leverage: demand energy certificate, building reserve status, recent maintenance history, terrace waterproofing documentation, and exact floor/orientation; price chips away fast when those aren’t crisp.

Bottom line

This looks like a "sell the terrace" apartment in a very transit-connected pocket of 1020. If the terrace is truly large/usable and the building is recent + well-run, it can be a great liveability/investment hybrid. If the terrace is awkward/windy/shaded or the building costs/reserve are messy, the €/sqm stops making sense fast.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely modern/renovated (floor heating helps), but the listing extract is missing the condition/energy/monthly-cost details that decide “plug-and-play” vs surprises.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (1 room / ~46 sqm is a lifestyle pad, not a family base) Investor: ➖ (strong central demand, but 1-room pricing needs rent/costs to pencil; HOA/repairs fund unknown) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (sunny + balcony + quiet-in-the-city is exactly the “Vienna weekday luxury” spec)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — prime-ish micro-area signals and balcony are a big plus, but €/sqm looks punchy for a 1-room unless the building/finish and running costs are clearly top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €455k–€475k (best-effort guess; true comps not provided). Push hardest on missing disclosures: monthly building costs, repair reserve, and any upcoming facade/roof/lift works. If the unit is truly quiet despite the address and has high-quality finishes, be ready to tighten the range upward; otherwise anchor lower.

Bottom line

Two real value-drivers here: balcony + “quiet while central” (rare combo). The risk is financial, not emotional: 46 sqm studios can be liquidity-sensitive if priced like a 2-room. Before you fall in love, demand the cost stack + building condition story in writing.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — looks like a new-build project listing, but the extract lacks finish/spec, delivery date, and exact unit data.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (could work if the exact unit is quiet + good layout, but 2nd district near major transit can be hit-or-miss for noise) Investor: ✔ (2nd district + strong transit/amenities usually rents well; “work + live” angle can broaden demand) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high amenity density and parks nearby; likely easy daily life without a car)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — area fundamentals look strong (parks + groceries + transit), but you’re buying blind right now: rooms/sqm/floor/spec are missing and those decide everything.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €460k–€485k (best-effort guess; comps and the unit’s sqm/room count are unknown). Push for concessions instead of only price if it’s a developer sale: upgrade package, parking/storage, or fee caps. Tie every ask to missing certainties: exact unit plan, orientation, floor, window spec, handover timeline, and total monthly running costs.

Bottom line

This is a “location is doing the heavy lifting” deal: parks + transit + groceries within a short walk is hard to beat. But without sqm/rooms/floor, €495k could be fair or wildly off — the unit details are the whole game. Two ways this goes wrong fast: you end up on a noisy exposure (arterial road/rail) or discover surprisingly high monthly costs/fees that kill resale and rental yield.

#392
PREMIUM ALTBAUWOHNUNG - ZENTRALE RUHELAGE - ERSTBEZUG - underfloor heating (PREMIUM ALTBAUWOHNUNG | ZENTRALE RUHELAGE | ERSTBEZUG| FUSSBODENHEIZUNG)
€495,000 · 61 m2 · 2 rooms · Mumbgasse 1-3, Vienna
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Address/map mismatch flagged (likely approximate pin).
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €495,000
  • Size: 61 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “first occupancy” + underfloor heating suggests turnkey, but the devil is in the building systems and orientation.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / 61 sqm can work for a couple + baby, tight for long-term) Investor: ✔ (strong micro-location signals; easy-to-rent size if layout is efficient) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (dense food/drink + parks + transit nearby without being purely tourist-core)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good size/price bracket for the 2nd district, but you need clarity on monthly running costs and the scope/quality of the renovation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €465k–€485k (best-effort guess; exact comps not provided). Levers: (1) request the full monthly cost breakdown + reserve fund status; if costs/reserves are weak, price adjusts. (2) pin down what “first occupancy” means in writing (full unit renovation vs partial refresh) and tie any unresolved punch-list to a retention or discount. (3) underfloor heating = comfort, but also a risk: ask for system type, warranty, commissioning docs; uncertainty here is a direct negotiating chip.

Bottom line

This is the classic “renovated older-building charm” pitch in a high-demand area — it can be a great home if the building is financially healthy. Two ways it goes wrong: surprise high monthly costs from weak reserves / upcoming common-area works; or a technically messy renovation (noise from neighbors, heating performance issues, dampness) that you only discover after moving in. If the unit faces a quiet inner courtyard and the building paperwork is clean, it’s a strong buy at the right number.

Depends — titled as “well maintained”, but key unknowns (floor, heating, energy, monthly building costs) can flip the true carrying cost and comfort.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3 rooms/70 sqm can work, but layout + floor/lift + noise near market are decisive unknowns) Investor: ✔ (2nd district + strong amenity/transit density supports liquidity and rental demand) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Karmelitermarkt area is one of the most liveable, walkable pockets of Vienna)

Final verdict

Bottom line

This is a “buy the neighborhood” listing — the amenity density is exactly what people pay for. If the building costs are sane and the apartment isn’t facing a loud street, it’s an easy yes. If costs are high or the unit is dark/noisy, your only win is price — otherwise walk.

  • Size: 53.8 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — underfloor heating is a strong “modernized/new-build” signal, but with no condition/energy info you’re buying partially blind.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (53.8 sqm / 2 rooms can work for a couple + baby, but it’s tight long-term) Investor: ➖ (high €/sqm implies yield pressure; could work if demand for this micro-location stays hot) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (if you want peak-city, walk-everywhere Vienna and don’t mind buzz)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location looks prime-and-lively, but the price level and missing operating/energy details make this a “verify hard, then bid” situation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €490k–€510k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat this as a starting anchor, not a truth). Push for full monthly costs, reserve fund status, building works plan, and an energy certificate; if any are weak/unknown, price that risk in.

Bottom line

You’re paying for micro-location: canal-side city energy, parks nearby, and strong everyday convenience. Main risk is livability vs. buzz: noise/foot-traffic and “always-on” surroundings can be a dealbreaker if you’re noise-sensitive. At ~€9.7k/sqm, the apartment needs to be genuinely high-spec (or have some unique moat) to justify the ticket.

  • Size: 46.39 m2
  • Rooms: 2

  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new-build first occupancy vibes), but “depends” on handover quality and snag list.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (46.39 sqm / 2 rooms is tight long-term) Investor: ➖ (good liquidity if location is strong; price-per-sqm risk) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (balcony + underfloor heating + central-ish 2nd district energy)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — nice product, but at ~€551k for 46 sqm you’re paying a premium; make the building/area realities earn that price.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €510k–€540k. Levers: (1) treat “first occupancy” as a snag-risk—price in punch-list items + delays; (2) ask for clarity on building reserve status and any near-term special contributions; (3) pin down exact balcony orientation/noise exposure before offering.

Bottom line

This is a “high finish, small footprint” buy: you’re mainly buying location + building-newness, not space. If the balcony faces quiet/open and the building finances are clean, it’s a strong lifestyle apartment. If it faces traffic/rail or the HOA costs land high, the resale/rent math gets ugly fast.

€552,000 · 46.48 m2 · 2 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €552,000
  • Size: 46.48 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new-build first occupancy); the “depends” is what the developer actually delivers vs. the spec.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / ~46.5 sqm is tight unless it’s just a couple + baby) Investor: ➖ (works as a premium rental, but price-per-sqm is aggressive; yields depend on final total monthly costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (balcony + underfloor heating + central-ish 1020 vibe is a strong daily-life combo)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — nice product, but at €552k for ~46.5 sqm you’re paying “new-build + marketing + developer margin”; only buy if the micro-location/noise/orientation checks out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €510k–€540k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat this as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Levers: (1) ask for the full breakdown of monthly running costs + reserve fund contributions (a lot of “new-build sticker shock” hides here), (2) confirm whether any developer incentives exist (kitchen/wardrobes, parking/storage, closing-cost contribution), (3) use uncertainty on floor/orientation/noise as a price lever until clarified in writing.

Bottom line

You’re buying “convenience + newness” more than space. The area signal looks good for walkability (parks/transit density), but 1020 can swing wildly block-to-block on noise. If this is a lifestyle buy, prioritize quiet orientation and resale liquidity over squeezing an extra 2–3k off the price.

€595,000 · 100.34 m2 · 5 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €595,000
  • Size: 100.34 m2
  • Rooms: 5
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “period building” + “development potential” usually means charm with at least some functional/technical upgrades likely.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (5 rooms / ~100 sqm reads like flexible family layout, if the floorplan isn’t too chopped up) Investor: ➖ (big unit in a good micro-area can rent well, but renovation capex + old-building unknowns can kill yield) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Pratercottage vibe + walkable amenities; you’re buying atmosphere as much as space)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location signal and rare room count, but the listing itself is basically advertising “work needed,” so the price has to reflect renovation + old-building risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €540k–€580k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Anchor on: “development potential” wording, missing hard specs (floor/heating/energy), and monthly costs (€428.8) relative to size. Ask for: full cost breakdown (reserve fund, lift, heating), recent building works, and any planned façade/roof/risers projects.

Bottom line

This is a “buy the address + volume, then shape it” apartment. If the building is well-run and the layout is usable, 5 rooms near Pratercottage is a scarcity play. If the building needs major works or the apartment needs a full gut reno, you want a meaningfully lower entry price to keep the story rational.

€599,400 · 122.53 m2 · 4 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €599,400
  • Size: 122.53 m2
  • Rooms: 4
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — the “wohnprojekt / project” wording plus “modern attic” vibe often means either a fresh conversion or a unit that’s still being finalized; you’ll need clarity on finish level, delivery date, and what exactly is included.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ Big enough on paper (4 rooms / 122 sqm), but roof-level layouts can be quirky; check bedroom sizing, storage, and noise/heat management. Investor: ➖ At ~€4.9k/sqm it’s not crazy for Vienna, but 4-room liquidity depends heavily on micro-location and the building’s legal/technical story (condo split, reserves, any planned works). Lifestyle buyer: ✔ Leopoldstadt with strong walk-to-food/transit density reads as “city life with oxygen” — good if you want cafés, parks, and quick connections.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the size/price ratio is compelling, but “period building + Dachgeschoss” is where hidden costs and comfort risks live; buy only after the building + conversion details check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €540k–€585k (comps unknown, so this is a risk-adjusted bid range). Use uncertainty as leverage: roof conversion specs, energy performance, ongoing building works, and monthly running costs. Ask for: full spec sheet, plans with room dimensions, building reserve status, and a written list of what’s new vs. legacy (roof, insulation, windows, elevator, risers).

Bottom line

2nd district + lots of amenities nearby is a real quality-of-life advantage. This is a “paper winner, diligence loser” category unless the attic build quality and building finances are pristine. If the conversion is top-tier and heat/noise are solved, you could end up with a standout home; if not, you’ll feel every compromise daily.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely fine to live in, but “individual heating” can hide comfort/cost surprises until you see the system type, age, and recent bills.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (102.4 sqm / 4 rooms in 1020 is a real, usable layout if the plan is sane) Investor: ➖ (good demand, but price level + unknown monthly costs make yield math uncertain) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Prater adjacency + strong transit + lots of daily-life amenities nearby)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive size/location combo, but too many “unknowns” (floor, operating costs, energy, heating details) to pay top euro without de-risking.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €600,000–€625,000 (best-effort; comps unknown). Use the uncertainty stack as leverage: missing floor info, no monthly costs, no energy class, and the extra due diligence burden from individual heating. Bottom line

You’re paying for livability: parks + transit density is excellent, and the area has real day-to-day infrastructure. Biggest risk is not the neighborhood — it’s building/comfort economics (heating + monthly costs) and the missing basics that can swing value fast.

#403
ALLES NEU! Super geschnittene 4-Z-apartment in metro-Naehe! (ALLES NEU! Super geschnittene 4-Z-Wohnung in U-Bahn-Nähe!)
€649,000 · 84 m2 · 4 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Address/map mismatch flagged (likely approximate pin).
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €649,000
  • Size: 84 m2
  • Rooms: 4
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “all new” suggests turnkey finishes, but the missing floor/building details + individual heating (unit-level heating) are the risk flags to verify.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (84 sqm / 4 rooms in the 2nd can work well if layout is genuinely efficient) Investor: ➖ (strong location signals, but heating type + unknown building/floor can cap tenant appeal and resale liquidity) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (very walkable micro-area; lots of food/drink + transit density)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location/micro-area looks strong, but at ~€7.7k/sqm you need proof on building quality, floor/light/noise, and what “new” actually includes.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €600k–€630k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Use the uncertainty points as leverage: individual heating running cost/comfort, unknown floor (light/noise), and any gaps in energy/operating-cost transparency. Ask for: renovation scope/invoices, heating age & consumption history, building reserve status, and a clear monthly-cost breakdown.

Bottom line

This smells like a high-convenience Leopoldstadt buy: restaurants, groceries, parks, and transit all right there. You’re paying “renovated + great layout” money — make sure you’re not buying a pretty interior in a mediocre building. If the floor/light/noise check out, it’s a strong live-in option; if not, the price needs to move.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Address/map mismatch flagged (likely approximate pin).
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €650,000
  • Size: 73 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: Luftwaermepumpe
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely fine on fundamentals, but the listing data is thin (no floor, no energy class, no monthly costs, no condition text), so treat it as “needs verification” before assuming turnkey.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / 73 sqm can work for a couple + baby, but it’s not a long-horizon family layout) Investor: ➖ (strong location liquidity, but unknown running costs + no details on condition; yield may be tight at this price) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (central 1020 vibe with lots of food/drink + parks + transit within 300m)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great micro-location signals and a modern-sounding heating system, but the missing hard facts (operating costs, floor/noise, building condition, energy, renovation) are exactly where expensive surprises live.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €610,000–€640,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) “private sale/no commission” shouldn’t inflate price, it should reduce total acquisition cost; (2) uncertainty discount for missing Betriebskosten/Ruecklage/energy info; (3) confirm heat pump setup and any building-level upgrades still pending.

Bottom line

If this is truly a quiet, well-run building in the inner 1020 pocket, it’s a very livable 2-room. But you’re buying a *story* right now — force it to become a *documented fact set* before you pay “Innenstadtlage” premium. Prioritize: floor/exposure + noise + monthly costs + reserve fund + any upcoming building works.

€695,000 · 73.9 m2 · 2 rooms · Vienna, 02. District, Leopoldstadt
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €695,000
  • Size: 73.9 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: Solar
  • District: Vienna 2

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — marketed as first occupancy, but rooftop/balcony details and building handover quality can swing from “perfect” to snag-list hell.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms + roof-terrace lifestyle; workable for a couple, tight for kids) Investor: ➖ (high ticket for 2 rooms; could rent well if micro-location is prime, but yield likely thin) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (DG + balcony + panoramic roof terrace = trophy-feel if noise/overheating are controlled)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the product is premium (DG/terrace/solar), but at ~€695k you must price in terrace maintenance, summer heat risk, and resale sensitivity.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €635k–€675k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted anchor rather than a comp-driven valuation). Push hardest on: (1) missing monthly costs + reserve fund info, (2) roof/terrace warranty scope, (3) summer comfort (solar gain) proof. If they can’t document these cleanly, you’re buying uncertainty — demand a discount.

Bottom line

This is a “buy the view” DG unit in a very walkable pocket (restaurants + groceries + transit density reads strong). The deal goes wrong if the terrace is a liability (leaks, special assessments) or if the apartment is a sauna in July. If orientation/noise and building finances check out, it’s a high-joy home — but don’t pay full trophy price for unknowns.

#SQ - RUHIGE SANIERUNGSBEDUeRFTIGE GARCONNIERE MITTEN IM 6ten /HAUS DES MEERES - TOP 1 (#SQ - RUHIGE SANIERUNGSBEDÜRFTIGE GARCONNIERE MITTEN IM 6ten /HAUS DES MEERES - TOP 1)
€199,000 · 36.68 m2 · 1 rooms · Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €199,000
  • Size: 36.68 m2
  • Rooms: 1
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “sanierungsbeduerftig” reads like a real renovation, not just paint.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (1 room / micro size) Investor: ✔ (strong central rental demand, but renovation + building unknowns matter) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (amazing location, but only if you genuinely want tiny-city living)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location is A-tier, but the renovation risk and missing cost/energy/building details should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €175k–€190k (best-effort guess; no comps provided, and the renovation scope is unknown). Push on: (1) renovation cost uncertainty, (2) unknown monthly running costs/reserve fund, (3) energy/heating unknown, (4) floor/light/noise details not specified even though it’s “quiet,” (5) any building-wide works (roof/façade/lift) that could trigger special assessments. Offer structure: price haircut OR seller contribution/credit for renovation; insist on documentation before removing contingencies.

Bottom line

You’re buying the Mariahilf/Haus des Meeres micro-area first, the apartment second. If the building is healthy and the unit has decent light, this can be a killer compact base. If it’s dark/ground-floor-ish or the building needs big capex, this turns into an expensive little box fast.

#407
2 rooms zentrale City-apartment direkt an der metro (2 Zimmer zentrale City-Wohnung direkt an der U-Bahn)
€225,000 · 46 m2 · 2 rooms · Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €225,000
  • Size: 46 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: central heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely fine for immediate living, but the real story hinges on noise/orientation, building condition, and what the monthly costs include.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (46 sqm / 2 rooms is tight long-term) Investor: ✔ (prime transit adjacency + strong rental liquidity, if noise/HOA risks are controlled) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walkable, restaurant-heavy micro-area, great “live small, live central” vibe)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location is doing most of the work; make sure you’re not overpaying for “metro next door” while inheriting noise, weak reserves, or expensive common-area issues.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €205k–€220k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Anchor with uncertainty: confirm what the €287.39/month covers (reserve fund vs operating vs heating), and press for price protection if building reserves are thin or upcoming works are planned. Concrete lever: make the offer conditional on written confirmation of reserve fund level + planned building works timeline.

Bottom line

This is a classic “tiny, central, transit-optimized” buy — great daily convenience, but you pay for it in potential noise and lower serenity. Your downside isn’t the unit size; it’s (1) sound/vibration from the line/street and (2) surprise building costs that make the monthly outlay feel like a bigger apartment. If the unit is quiet and the building finances are healthy, it’s a very defensible entry into a top-demand area.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (central + transit is great, but main-road noise/space uncertainty makes it a gamble) Investor: ✔ (liquid micro-location; likely strong rental demand, but yield depends entirely on unit details) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (if you want walk-everywhere city living and can tolerate Mariahilfer’s intensity)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — premium address, but without unit size/floor/fees/energy data you should treat €240k as “marketing from-price” until proven otherwise.

Bottom line

This is “hyper-central, hyper-convenient” Vienna living. The trade-off is intensity: crowding, retail noise, and traffic vibes unless you’re high-floor and courtyard-facing. Treat it as a micro-location win, unit-quality question mark — do not commit without the exact unit plan + orientation.

Target price range: €235k–€250k (best-effort guess; without comparable sales and without condition/energy data, the discount is mainly for uncertainty + potential capex). Ask for: (1) exact floor + courtyard vs street orientation, (2) renovation year + photo set, (3) building condition + planned works (roof/façade/lift), (4) energy certificate, (5) heating system age, (6) current tenancy status + rent details if occupied. Leverage: gas heating + missing energy class + unknown floor/lift = price risk; use this to push for a clean reduction or seller-funded fixes.

Bottom line

This is a prime “live central” micro-area with absurd convenience: restaurants/groceries/transit are off the charts. The main threat is not the neighborhood — it’s the apartment specifics (noise, light, layout, building capex) that aren’t disclosed. Treat it as a location buy, and price in a renovation buffer until you see evidence otherwise.

#411
GELDANLAGE II BEFRISTET VERMIETET BIS 01/2028 II 3 rooms apartment II GUTE LAGE NAeHE GUMPENDORFER Street (GELDANLAGE II BEFRISTET VERMIETET BIS 01/2028 II 3 ZIMMER WOHNUNG II GUTE LAGE NÄHE GUMPENDORFER STRASSE)
€289,000 · 62.42 m2 · 3 rooms · Mollardgasse, Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €289,000
  • Size: 62.42 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s currently rented until 01/2028, so “ready” only if you’re buying a tenant-in-place product.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (size is fine, but the locked-in tenancy limits flexibility) Investor: ✔ (clear investment framing + central-ish demand pocket) Lifestyle buyer: ❌ (you’re not living there for ~2 years unless something changes)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good 6th-district micro-location and 3 rooms at a sharp headline price, but the value hinges on lease terms and net yield after €335.19/mo costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €260k–€280k (best-effort guess; I don’t have rent amount, lease terms, or comps here, so this is primarily a risk-discount range). Push points: (1) fixed tenancy until 01/2028 = illiquidity/own-use penalty, (2) individual heating can mean higher maintenance/upgrade risk, (3) monthly costs reduce net yield — ask for rent roll + operating cost breakdown + reserve fund status.

Bottom line

If you want a “set-and-hold” city investment, this micro-area has the amenities/transit density to support stable demand. If your plan includes moving in soon, skip it — the tenant timeline dominates the decision. Do not price it off €/m² alone; price it off verified net cashflow and lease risk.

  • Size: 74.33 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: Gasheizung
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — sounds like an “investment pitch” listing; without condition/photos/energy data, assume you’ll need at least cosmetic work and due diligence.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3 rooms helps, but Mariahilfer Street can mean noise/crowds; layout and windows matter) Investor: ✔ (district 6, strong amenity/transit density, likely steady tenant demand) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere, restaurants, shops, transit; very urban living)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — micro-location is top-tier for demand, but missing building/condition/energy details + gas heating risk means you should price in uncertainty.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €285k–€305k (best-effort guess; true comps unknown from provided data). Push for (1) full operating cost breakdown and reserve status, (2) building/roof/façade/lift plans, (3) energy/heating documentation, (4) proof of rentability if they pitch “capital investment.” If anything is unclear, keep your offer conditional on document review.

Bottom line

You’re buying location-first: excellent city connectivity and amenities. The main risks are “unknowns” (condition, energy performance, future building works) and gas exposure. If the documents check out, this is the kind of place that stays liquid; if not, you’ll overpay for the address.

  • Size: 43.21 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — too many basics are missing (floor, running costs, energy certificate, actual condition), so “bright” could still mean “needs work.”

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (43 sqm, practical ceiling) Investor: ➖ (strong liquidity in 1060, but heating + noise risk can hurt yields) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere micro-area; tons of food/transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location is the whole point here, but the data gaps + individual heating can turn this into an expensive “cute on paper” deal.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €300k–€315k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps for this exact street/building). Your lever is verifiable uncertainty: demand Betriebskosten/Ruecklage + last house meeting notes + energy certificate; if they can’t show low running costs and a healthy reserve, push price or walk.

Bottom line

This is a classic 1060 “small-but-central” buy: you’re paying for intensity and convenience, not for peace. Two things decide if it’s a win: (1) quietness in the unit (orientation/windows), (2) monthly burn (heating + building costs). If both check out, it’s a very livable base; if either fails, resale/rent becomes a headache.

Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View

  • Size: 43.21 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely fine if cosmetically maintained, but “individual heating” + missing energy/floor details is a classic hidden-cost zone.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ Too small (43 sqm / 2 rooms) unless you’re truly minimalist. Investor: ➖ Great demand location, but yield may be tight at this entry price and unknown running costs. Lifestyle buyer: ✔ Prime “walk everywhere” base right off the main shopping/cafe spine.

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — micro-location is excellent, but information gaps (floor, energy, monthly costs) should translate into price leverage.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €300k–€315k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push hard on: documented monthly costs (incl. reserve fund), heating system age/efficiency, and any upcoming building works. If the building is older and the unit runs on individual gas, treat that as a negotiation item, not a footnote.

Bottom line

You’re buying the neighborhood: huge amenity density, strong transit, always-liquid demand. At 43 sqm, every layout flaw hurts — insist on a plan and noise check (street/inner courtyard). Great city pad if the building is solid; mediocre deal if reserves/maintenance are weak.

€331,000 · 81.71 m2 · 2 rooms · Mariahilfer Strasse 127, Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €331,000
  • Size: 81.71 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: Gasheizung
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — the listing gives almost zero interior/condition detail; assume you’ll need a proper viewing + scope check.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (big 2-room for Vienna, but Mariahilfer Street is loud/busy and less “kid-easy” day to day) Investor: ✔ (central corridor, strong rental liquidity; but returns depend heavily on condition + layout + noise) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (if you want walk-everywhere city living and don’t mind the hustle)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location is prime and liquid, but the “capital investment” framing + missing condition details usually means you must price in noise, building state, and potential CapEx.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €300k–€320k (best-effort guess; I do not have comps or condition photos, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) street noise + window quality, (2) layout efficiency for 81.7 sqm / 2 rooms, (3) building/roof/facade status and any upcoming major works, (4) heating (gas) and future retrofit risk, (5) whether monthly costs include meaningful reserves. Make your offer conditional on: full building documents review + a daytime and evening noise check.

Bottom line

This is “Vienna retail-core real estate”: insanely convenient, always in demand, and rarely peaceful. If the unit faces the street and the windows are average, you negotiate hard or you walk. If it’s courtyard-facing with solid windows and a clean building, it can be a very durable hold.

  • Heating: Radiatoren

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated) — but "period building" means verify building-side items, not just the unit finish.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms / 57 sqm works for a couple + baby, but long-term space is tight) Investor: ➖ (good liquidity location, but rentability depends heavily on floor/orientation/noise) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere Mariahilf vibe; dense food/transit around you)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location and a clean "renovated period building" pitch, but the main street address + unknown floor/operating costs can flip this from gem to headache.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: floor + orientation (street vs courtyard), windows (age + sound rating), heating system details, building reserve status (Ruecklage) and planned works, and the full operating cost breakdown (BK + lift + reserve + admin). Target price range (best-effort guess; comps unknown): €350k–€370k (closer to €350k if low floor / street-facing / noisy; closer to €370k if higher floor or courtyard-quiet and documentation is clean).

Bottom line

You’re paying for Mariahilf convenience: restaurants, groceries, parks, and transit density. This deal wins if it’s quiet and the building is financially healthy. It loses if it’s street-loud with weak windows or if the building is heading into expensive works that your monthly costs will quietly absorb.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new-build/first-occupancy positioning), but “ready” depends on what’s actually included (kitchen, lighting, terrace finish).

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (2 rooms / 56 sqm is tight long-term) Investor: ➖ (strong tenant demand in 1060, but watch yield vs new-build pricing and monthly costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (courtyard-quiet + terrace + walkable, restaurant-heavy micro-area)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is attractive (terrace + inner-courtyard + Mariahilf), but at this size the price needs to be justified by real “quiet”, terrace usability, and fit-out quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €365k–€385k (best-effort guess; exact comps not provided). Anchor on unknown floor/light, terrace orientation/size, and any missing fit-out items; request full cost breakdown (reserve, elevator/management, heating allocation) and confirm whether monthly costs include reserve.

Bottom line

If the courtyard is genuinely silent and the terrace is actually usable (sun + privacy + size), this is a very livable 1060 base. If it’s ground-floor-ish, shaded, or the terrace faces a service yard, the “terrace premium” evaporates. Price-per-sqm feels new-build-ish; you’re paying for convenience and location—make sure you’re not also buying compromises (light, noise, finishing gaps).

Family: ➖ (2 rooms can work for a couple + baby, but renovation + layout unknown) Investor: ➖ (good location demand, but renovation capex + building/heating uncertainties can crush yield) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (1060/Mariahilf is a high-convenience, walk-everywhere district if you value city life)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong macro/micro location, but you’re buying a construction project with several unknowns (floor, condition scope, heating/energy).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess, comps unknown): €340k–€375k. Use the uncertainty as leverage: (1) renovation scope (electrics/plumbing/windows), (2) individual heating risk (gas dependency / future replacement), (3) missing monthly costs + missing energy class, (4) building condition/reserves. Make your offer contingent on getting the house fund/reserve status and recent building works documented.

Bottom line

If the building is solid and the unit has good light + quiet orientation, this could be a classic “buy the worst unit in the best street” play. But if it’s low floor / street-facing / gas-heavy with weak reserves, you’ll bleed money and time. Your edge is treating this like a spreadsheet + site inspection, not a romantic period building dream.

Move-in readiness

Depends — costs look manageable, but the listing signals “investment positioning” more than clear condition/renovation transparency.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2 rooms can work for a couple + baby, but it’s a high-activity high-street setup) Investor: ✔ (strong tenant demand dynamics; easy “sell the story” location) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (amazing convenience; trade-off is noise/crowds and less neighborhood calm)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — location is prime for liquidity and rental demand, but the price-per-sqm feels “retail-street premium” and the lack of condition/energy details is a leverage gift.

Negotiation strategy

Start with the information asymmetry: request renovation history, windows orientation (street vs courtyard), noise profile, and any building capex pipeline. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps unknown): €355k–€385k. If it’s courtyard-facing + good windows/insulation, you can justify closer to €385k; if street-facing/noisy or needs work, push toward €355k.

Bottom line

This is a “liquidity play” in a top-demand corridor: easy to rent, easy to resell. Your main risks are livability (noise) and hidden building/energy inefficiency costs. Buy it only if the unit’s orientation + condition confirm it’s not a forever-compromise apartment.

Move-in ready (fresh renovation + first occupancy), but verify building-level condition and heating system details.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (43 sqm / 2 rooms is tight long-term) Investor: ➖ (excellent location liquidity, but small-unit pricing is unforgiving and running costs are unknown) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere, restaurants/cafes, transit, and a newly finished interior)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — prime micro-area and easy resale/rentability, but price per sqm is punchy and key facts (floor, lift, energy, monthly costs) are missing.

Negotiation strategy

Bottom line

You’re paying for location and “new feeling,” not space. Great as a city base or high-convenience home; mediocre if you want quiet, storage, or future-proofing. Do not bid aggressively until you’ve seen the building, the courtyard/stairwell, and the real monthly numbers.

Move-in readiness

Depends — “modern” + underfloor heating suggests turnkey, but the main street address raises noise/air-quality questions you must verify on-site.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3 rooms but only ~53.7 sqm; works for one child max, storage will be tight) Investor: ✔ (6th district liquidity + extreme amenity/transit density; easy to rent/sell, but tenant noise-sensitivity risk) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (if you want walk-everywhere city living and don’t mind Mariahilfer Street energy)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great location fundamentals, but the price-per-sqm and “main artery” trade-offs need a discount unless it’s exceptionally quiet/optimized.

Negotiation strategy

Push on value-per-sqm (≈ €8.2k/sqm) and street exposure; make the offer conditional on (1) noise test at peak hours, (2) window spec, (3) building maintenance/reserve fund clarity. Target price range: €405k–€425k (best-effort; I don’t have comps for the exact micro-location/condition).

Bottom line

This is a “buy the location” apartment: unbeatable daily convenience, strong resale. You’re paying for Mariahilfer Street prestige — make sure you’re not also buying its noise. At ~54 sqm, layout quality matters more than the room count: measure bedrooms, storage, and light before you fall in love.

Depends — likely fine to live in, but “individual heating” often means older in-unit systems and higher variance in comfort + running costs.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (82 sqm can work, but “2 rooms / 2.5” reads more couple/small-family; near Westbahnhof can be noisy) Investor: ✔ (liquid location; rental demand tends to be resilient around major transit) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (dense food/drink + parks + transit screams walkable city life)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great micro-location for daily life and exit liquidity, but the price only makes sense if the building condition, noise exposure, and heating system check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €485k–€505k (comps unknown — treat this as a leverage anchor, not a valuation). Push hardest on: (1) missing monthly costs, (2) lack of energy class, (3) any upcoming building works, (4) heating age/service history, (5) window quality + street-facing noise.

Bottom line

You’re buying location first: transit-rich, restaurant-rich, very “liveable Vienna.” Near Westbahnhof is a double-edged sword: convenience + resale, but noise/air + tourist/transit churn. Make the deal hinge on hard facts: operating costs, building reserve/repairs, and whether it’s quiet enough for your lifestyle.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking

  • Price: €549,000
  • Size: 89.78 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: individual heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely livable as-is, but “floor unknown + missing energy/heating details” means budget for inspection surprises.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good size for 3 rooms, but Westbahnhof-adjacent noise/traffic risk) Investor: ➖ (strong rental demand area, but micro-noise + operating-cost opacity can cap yield) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walkable, restaurant-heavy, transit-saturated, genuinely central)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location is a demand engine, but the listing data is thin where it matters (floor, running costs, energy, condition).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €510k–€535k (best-effort guess; comps unknown from the provided data). Anchor with (1) individual heating operating-cost risk, (2) ask for Hausgeld/Ruecklage and recent building works, (3) noise check at peak rail/road times, (4) any upcoming façade/roof/lift projects.

Bottom line

2 things are doing the heavy lifting here: 90 sqm / 3 rooms (rarely feels cramped) and a hyper-walkable Mariahilf pocket. But Westbahnhof proximity is a double-edged sword: convenience vs. noise, foot traffic, and air quality. Treat this like a “location-first” buy: if the building is solid and the windows are good, it’s a keeper; if not, you’ll feel it daily.

#428
period building-Charme in Toplage beim Haus des Meeres (Altbau-Charme in Toplage beim Haus des Meeres)
€549,000 · Esterhazygasse 28, Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €549,000
  • Heating: district heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build project + “old-building charm” marketing usually means the shell/location are great, but your real readiness hinges on what exactly is being delivered (turnkey vs. “prepare to renovate”) and the building’s common parts.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (amazing amenities, but the micro-location can be loud/late-night) Investor: ✔ (liquidity is strong here; rentability depends heavily on layout + legal status) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere, restaurants, transit, city energy)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location near Haus des Meeres is a premium, but without sqm/room count/energy + running costs this is a “price-to-spec” situation; don’t pay for vibes.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €505k–€535k (best-effort guess; true comps are unknown without sqm, condition, and exact deliverables). Your leverage is the missing/unclear fundamentals: demand a full spec sheet (net sqm, room count, floor, lift, courtyard/street orientation, condition/renovation scope, monthly costs) and make the offer contingent on those matching a reasonable €/sqm for this micro-area.

Bottom line

This is one of Vienna’s most “daily-life efficient” pockets: food/drink density is insane, groceries are close, and transit is strong. The trade-off is noise + tourist/foot-traffic spillover — great if you want city buzz, punishing if you expect calm. If the unit is well-oriented (quiet side/courtyard) and the building economics are clean, it can be a very solid hold.

Move-in ready — “top renovated” + district heating suggests low near-term capex risk, but verify what was actually replaced (electrics/plumbing/windows).

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3 rooms at ~82 sqm works, but unknown floor/lift + garden is “shared” not private) Investor: ➖ (strong 1060 demand helps, but parking value depends on access/ownership; check rentability rules and costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walkable Mariahilf vibe + food/transit density + parking is a rare convenience combo)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — location is doing a lot of work here; without floor/lift/sunlight/noise proof it’s hard to pay “renovation premium” at full ask.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €515k–€540k (best-effort; true comps unknown). Anchor on missing key facts (floor/lift, orientation, window spec), monthly costs, and whether the parking spot is deeded or just a usage right.

If the renovation is real (not cosmetic) and the unit is bright/quiet, this is a very livable 1060 home. Parking + shared garden can be a genuine quality-of-life upgrade in Mariahilf — but only if the access/legal setup is clean. Do not commit before you understand floor, lift, and street-side noise exposure.

#430
Ruhige, sanierte 3,5 rooms apartment mit Designer-Bad und Kueche + Garage (optional!) in Mariahilf - beziehbar ab 01/26! (Ruhige, sanierte 3,5 Zimmer Wohnung mit Designer-Bad und Küche + Garage (optional!) in Mariahilf - beziehbar ab 01/26!)
€550,000 · 90 m2 · 3 rooms · Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €550,000
  • Size: 90 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — described as renovated with “designer” kitchen/bath and underfloor heating, but you can’t actually move in until 01/26.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (size is good, but 3 rooms / “3.5” nuance + delayed availability makes timing tricky) Investor: ✔ (Mariahilf liquidity + high amenity density; but budget carefully for high monthly costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (quiet-renovated vibe in a very walkable, restaurant/transit-rich pocket)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location + renovation story, but the 01/26 availability and €554/mo running costs are leverage and should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €510k–€535k (best-effort guess; no comps provided, so treat this as a positioning range, not a valuation). Ask for a clean breakdown of the €553.98/mo (repairs reserve, lift, heating/hot water, any special levies), proof of renovation scope (invoices/warranties), and clarify the exact “3.5 rooms” layout + floor/quiet-side orientation. Use the delayed handover to push either price down or seller-paid costs until handover.

Bottom line

Mariahilf + “quiet” + renovated is a rare combo and usually rents/sells well. The two red flags to underwrite are: (1) you’re buying today for a 2026 move-in, and (2) the monthly costs are chunky for 90 sqm. If the layout is genuinely flexible (proper third bedroom/office) and the building is healthy, this can be a very livable long-term hold.

Freier MZ: Ruhige 3-rooms-apartment mit Loggia nahe Mariahilfer Street ***befristet vermietet*** (Freier MZ: Ruhige 3-Zimmer-Wohnung mit Loggia nahe Mariahilfer Straße ***befristet vermietet***)
€598,000 · 75 m2 · 3 rooms · Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €598,000
  • Size: 75 m2
  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: district heating
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s advertised as fixed-term rented, so your actual move-in timing/control is the main unknown.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good size for a small family, but loggia + tenancy timing + 75 sqm limits “grow into it”) Investor: ✔ (tenanted unit + district heating + Mariahilf demand profile can work if rent/terms aren’t weird) Lifestyle buyer: ❌ (Mariahilf lifestyle is great, but being rented undermines the whole point)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location near Mariahilfer Street, but you’re paying a premium while accepting tenancy/hand-over uncertainty.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €540k–€575k (best-effort; comps unknown). Push hard on (1) exact remaining lease term + notice/termination mechanics, (2) current rent and indexation, (3) handover condition at end of lease, and (4) whether monthly costs include a meaningful reserve fund. If the lease runs “long” or is tenant-friendly, treat it like an investment product and price it off yield, not vibes.

Bottom line

Mariahilf + quiet + loggia is a rare combo — the area is high-convenience, high-liquidity. The “befristet vermietet” tag is not a footnote: it’s the whole deal. At ~€8k/sqm headline, you need either a discount or exceptionally clean tenancy economics to make this feel smart.

Move-in readiness

Depends — “luxury, furnished” usually means turnkey, but with no description, floor, or running costs disclosed, you’re buying vibes not verification.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (62.56 sqm / 2 rooms in a very urban patch) Investor: ➖ (strong rental demand area, but furniture-heavy “luxury” units can be harder to comp and maintain) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (District 6 with extreme amenity density; you’ll actually use the neighborhood)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — location is a cheat code, but the missing fundamentals (floor, operating costs, energy/condition) are exactly where expensive surprises hide.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €540k–€580k (best-effort guess; comps unknown from provided data). Push for a discount tied to (1) undisclosed monthly costs, (2) unknown floor/noise/daylight, and (3) furniture value being marketing, not bankable property value. Make your offer conditional on complete documents (Betriebskosten breakdown, repair reserve, energy info, and a clear inventory list for furnishings).

Bottom line

This is a classic “buy the zip code” play: ridiculous convenience (food/drink=97, transit=16, parks=10 within ~300m). At ~€9.6k/sqm you must be convinced on light, noise, and building quality — otherwise you’re overpaying for interior styling. Treat furniture as a nice extra, not the reason to pay top-of-market.

  • Rooms: 3
  • Heating: Gasheizung
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — reads like a “character” unit with upside, but assume at least some refurbishment/modernization and clarify condition on a viewing.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (big sqm helps, but Mariahilfer Street frontage/noise and layout details will decide) Investor: ➖ (area is liquid, but gas heating + older stock risk and unknown floor/lift can cap yield) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (central, walk-everywhere, huge amenities density)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great location and size, but the ad’s “creative possibilities” plus gas heating and missing basics (floor/energy class) mean you buy only at the right price.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: exact floor + lift, windows orientation (street vs courtyard), noise levels, renovation history (electrics/plumbing), building works planned, and a complete energy cert. Target price range: €560k–€595k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, and Mariahilfer Street can swing value hard depending on orientation/condition).

Bottom line

102.9 sqm in the 6th with extreme walkability is the core value here. If it’s street-facing or needs major work, it’s a discount play — don’t pay “turnkey” money. If it’s quiet-courtyard and structurally solid, it’s a long-term keeper even if you renovate cosmetically.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (great size, but this micro-location tends to skew loud/traffic-heavy unless the unit is high up / well insulated) Investor: ✔ (strong rental/liquidity zone near a prime shopping/transit spine; easy to re-sell) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere living: lots of food/drink, parks, and transit density)

Final verdict

Bottom line

This is a classic “pay for location + view” 1060 play. If the apartment is high-floor, quiet, and properly insulated, it’s a lifestyle win. If it faces traffic/noise or has mediocre energy performance, you’ll feel it every day — and you should price that in.

#436
KRISENSICHERE WERTANLAGE! Vermietete Altbauwohnung in 1060 - Top-Lage! Ideale smarte Anlage! (KRISENSICHERE WERTANLAGE! Vermietete Altbauwohnung in 1060 - Top-Lage! Ideale smarte Anlage!)
€209,000 · 33.05 m2 · Vienna, 06. District, Mariahilf
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €209,000
  • Size: 33.05 m2
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s advertised as rented (“vermietete Altbauwohnung”), so your move-in timeline is controlled by the tenancy, not the condition.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ Too small (33 sqm) and tenancy reduces flexibility. Investor: ✔ Stable, easy-to-rent micro-location (dense transit + amenities) if yield and tenant terms check out. Lifestyle buyer: ❌ Great area, but this listing is explicitly positioned as an investment and likely not immediately usable.

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location carries, but at 33 sqm you’re buying a rental contract + building quality; without rent, lease terms, and building/maintenance clarity you should treat the ask as a starting point.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €185k–€205k (comps unknown; this range is a risk-adjusted bid given small size + rented status + missing key facts like rent level, lease duration, floor, and energy/heating). Use gaps as leverage: demand proof of current rent (net/gross), deposit, contract type (fixed vs open-ended), indexation clause, operating cost split, and any planned building works; price any capex risk (old building, potential facade/roof/stairwell works) directly into your offer.

Bottom line

If you want a “set-and-forget” micro-unit in a very liquid district, this can work — but only if the rent and tenant terms make the yield real. The biggest hidden variable isn’t the apartment; it’s the lease structure and building capex pipeline. Don’t pay prime-1060 pricing unless you can verify the income stream and the building is financially clean.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (65 sqm/3 rooms can work, but terrace + inner-6th usually means trade-offs on quiet/storage) Investor: ✔ (WG-friendly 3-room in 1060 tends to rent well if layout is truly shareable) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walkable core: restaurants + parks + transit density reads as “Vienna-city living”)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — excellent central micro-location for the money, but the missing basics (floor, building state, energy/renovation history) are exactly where expensive surprises live.

Negotiation strategy

This is a “buy the location” play: 1060 + strong amenity/transit counts is the real asset. At €319k it can still be good value, but only if the terrace is genuinely usable and the building/heat setup isn’t a future cash drain. Treat it like a diligence-heavy deal: verify the boring stuff before you fall in love with the terrace.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €594,000
  • District: Vienna 6

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “first occupancy / renovated” sounds plug-and-play, but the listing extract is missing the hard specs (size, floor, operating costs, energy), so readiness is only a marketing-level signal.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (2-room vibe + central location can work for a couple/young family, but without sqm/floor/lift it’s a gamble) Investor: ✔ (prime 6th + heavy amenity density suggests strong rental liquidity; verify yields vs monthly costs) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (this is classic “walk-everywhere” Vienna: lots of food/shops/parks/transit within 300m)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location is doing most of the work here; pay a premium only after you confirm fundamentals (sqm, floor, building condition, monthly costs, energy, noise).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess): €540k–€580k. Comps are unknown from the provided data, so treat this as a leverage anchor, not a valuation. Push for: full operating-cost breakdown (incl. reserves), energy/cooling/heating specifics, exact unit size + floorplan, and a noise/light check (street vs courtyard; “partly green view” can mean “mostly not”). If multiple 2-room units exist in the project, negotiate by comparing €/sqm across the stack.

Bottom line

This is a “buy the neighborhood” listing: Mariahilf convenience is real, and demand is typically deep. At ~€594k, you’re paying for prime livability—don’t do it blind; the missing sqm/floor/monthly costs can swing the deal from smart to overpriced. If the unit is quiet (courtyard) and the building finances are clean, it’s a strong lifestyle/investment hybrid.

  • Size: 60 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: central heating

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated claim), but terrace/roof + top-floor details should be verified carefully.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (2 rooms, 60 sqm, lifestyle layout) Investor: ➖ (strong area demand, but terrace/top-floor risk + tenant profile matters) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (top-floor feel + terrace + view in a prime inner district)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (terrace + top floor in District 6), but price-per-sqm and top-floor/roof unknowns mean you want leverage and paperwork.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €540k–€575k (best-effort estimate; exact comps not provided, so treat this as a negotiation anchor rather than a valuation). Push hardest on: (1) roof/terrace waterproofing history + warranties, (2) upcoming building works/reserve fund status, (3) summer overheating risk and any cooling measures, (4) elevator modernisation/maintenance, (5) total all-in monthly carrying costs beyond the stated €219.54. If garage is optional, separate it: don’t let it blur the apartment value.

Bottom line

This looks like a classic “urban trophy” 2-room: you’re buying vibe, light, and outdoor space. District 6 micro-location metrics scream walkable daily life (food/drink density, groceries, parks) — great for living, not always optimal for quiet. Top-floor terrace can be magic or a maintenance trap; make the roof paperwork decide the deal, not the photos.

The best version of central Vienna living is quiet-at-home but walkable-to-everything; the trade-offs are tourism gradient, event-driven crowding, and late-night noise. Treat courtyard vs street exposure and window quality as the real differentiators.

#442
+++ Hochwertig sanierte 2-rooms-apartment in Bestlage I 42 m2 I inkl. Lift. +++ (+++ Hochwertig sanierte 2-Zimmer-Wohnung in Bestlage I 42m2 I inkl. Lift. +++)
€329,000 · 42 m2 · 2 rooms · Vienna, 07. District, Neubau
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Address/map mismatch flagged (likely approximate pin).
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €329,000
  • Size: 42 m2
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 7

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated + underfloor heating suggests “done” rather than a project).

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (42 sqm / 2 rooms is tight long-term) Investor: ✔ (prime inner-city rental demand, low vacancy risk) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (walk-everywhere micro-area; great if you want city life)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — excellent location fundamentals, but 42 sqm at this price needs a clean quality/maintenance story to justify €/sqm.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: renovation scope/invoices, year of building + recent common-area works, reserve fund status, and any elevator/roof/façade plans; small units can get hit by building-wide capex. Target price range (best-effort): €305k–€320k (without comps this is a positioning guess; if the finish level is truly top-tier and building finances are strong, expect less flexibility).

Bottom line

This is “buy the neighborhood” territory: you’re paying for being in a dense, walkable, restaurant-and-transit-rich pocket. The main risk is not livability — it’s value discipline: small size means resale/rent math must be tight. If documents check out, it’s a strong city-base and a very liquid asset type.

#443
Ihr Investment in Bestlage! Befristet vermietete apartment in perfekter Lage und mit bester Ausstattung! Mitten im 7. district! (Ihr Investment in Bestlage! Befristet vermietete Wohnung in perfekter Lage und mit bester Ausstattung! Mitten im 7. Bezirk!)
€450,000 · 2 rooms · Mondscheingasse, Vienna, 07. District, Neubau
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/Parking
Ad (EN) | Ad · Maps · Street View
  • Price: €450,000
  • Rooms: 2
  • Heating: underfloor heating
  • District: Vienna 7

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — likely high-spec interior (underfloor heating), but it’s currently fixed-term rented so you’re buying a tenancy first, not a home.

Buyer fit

Family: ❌ (2 rooms + tenant-in-place limits flexibility) Investor: ✔ (this is explicitly positioned as an investment; low monthly operating costs help) Lifestyle buyer: ❌ (prime 7th-district location, but you can’t just move in)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the micro-location looks excellent, but without size/rent/contract details you’re blind on yield and on when (or if) you can personally use it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €410k–€440k (best-effort guess; comps and even sqm are unknown, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push for: (1) exact net sqm + floor + lift + orientation, (2) full rental terms (rent amount, indexation, deposit, end date, tenant break clauses), (3) proof of operating costs split and reserve fund, (4) any planned building works (roof/façade/lift) and current repair reserve status. If rent is below market or term is long, price should reflect the opportunity cost.

Bottom line

This is a “buy the location + buy the tenant” deal. The area metrics scream walkable, amenity-rich 7th (restaurants everywhere, solid groceries and transit). If the rent/term is not attractive, you’re effectively overpaying for a future option you can’t schedule.
